authored.para: This instruction selects consecutive elements from the two or four first source vectors where predicate elements are true, and places them in the corresponding elements of the two or four destination vectors. The corresponding elements from the two or four second source vectors are placed in the remaining elements of the destination vectors.

uses_dit.condition: FEAT_SVE2 is implemented or FEAT_SME is implemented

operationalnote_content.para: This instruction is a data-independent-time instruction as described in About PSTATE.DIT.

a.hover: For the "Two registers" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register of the destination multi-vector group, encoded as "Zd" times 2.
Is the size specifier,
For the "Consecutive" variant: is the name of the second scalable vector register of the destination multi-vector group, encoded as "Zd" times 2 plus 1.
Is the size specifier,
Is the name of the governing scalable predicate register PN8-PN15, with predicate-as-counter encoding, encoded in the "PNg" field.
For the "Two registers" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register of the first source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zn" times 2.
Is the size specifier,
Is the name of the second scalable vector register of the first source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zn" times 2 plus 1.
Is the size specifier,
For the "Two registers" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register of the second source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zm" times 2.
Is the size specifier,
Is the name of the second scalable vector register of the second source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zm" times 2 plus 1.
Is the size specifier,

ps.name: A64.sme.mortlach_multi_sve_1.mortlach_multi2_select_int.sel_mz_p_zz_2

a.hover: For the "Four registers" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register of the destination multi-vector group, encoded as "Zd" times 4.
Is the size specifier,
For the "Consecutive" variant: is the name of the fourth scalable vector register of the destination multi-vector group, encoded as "Zd" times 4 plus 3.
Is the size specifier,
Is the name of the governing scalable predicate register PN8-PN15, with predicate-as-counter encoding, encoded in the "PNg" field.
For the "Four registers" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register of the first source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zn" times 4.
Is the size specifier,
Is the name of the fourth scalable vector register of the first source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zn" times 4 plus 3.
Is the size specifier,
For the "Four registers" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register of the second source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zm" times 4.
Is the size specifier,
Is the name of the fourth scalable vector register of the second source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zm" times 4 plus 3.
Is the size specifier,

ps.name: A64.sme.mortlach_multi_sve_1.mortlach_multi4_select_int.sel_mz_p_zz_4

intro.para: For the "Two registers" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register of the destination multi-vector group, encoded as "Zd" times 2.
For the "Four registers" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register of the destination multi-vector group, encoded as "Zd" times 4.
Is the name of the second scalable vector register of the destination multi-vector group, encoded as "Zd" times 2 plus 1.
Is the name of the governing scalable predicate register PN8-PN15, with predicate-as-counter encoding, encoded in the "PNg" field.
For the "Two registers" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register of the first source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zn" times 2.
For the "Four registers" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register of the first source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zn" times 4.
Is the name of the second scalable vector register of the first source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zn" times 2 plus 1.
For the "Two registers" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register of the second source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zm" times 2.
For the "Four registers" variant: is the name of the first scalable vector register of the second source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zm" times 4.
Is the name of the second scalable vector register of the second source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zm" times 2 plus 1.
Is the name of the fourth scalable vector register of the destination multi-vector group, encoded as "Zd" times 4 plus 3.
Is the name of the fourth scalable vector register of the first source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zn" times 4 plus 3.
Is the name of the fourth scalable vector register of the second source multi-vector group, encoded as "Zm" times 4 plus 3.

ps.name: A64.sme.mortlach_multi_sve_1.mortlach_multi2_select_int.sel_mz_p_zz_2
